intro: Modesta mutabilis, also known as Garden Balsam or Balsam Poplar, is a deciduous tree species native to North America, characterized by its showy clusters of white or pink flowers. The plant has been traditionally used for its medicinal properties, particularly in treating respiratory issues such as bronchitis and coughs, due to its expectorant and anti-inflammatory effects. The bioactive constituents of Modesta mutabilis include flavonoids, phenolic acids, and terpenes, which contribute to its medicinal properties.
